findings.message: Phase 4 two-layer description saves to a file (.description.md) but user-spec Scenario G describes two-layer as a verbal explanation presented to the user during the session ('сначала объясняет на языке пользователя... затем техническое обоснование'). The implementation saves a file instead of structuring the spoken presentation. Tech-spec says 'two-layer descriptions in Phase 4' which is ambiguous. The file-based approach is a valid interpretation but slightly diverges from the conversational framing in user-spec.
findings.fix: Acceptable as-is given tech-spec framing. Optionally add a sentence in 4.2 that the Layer 1 summary should also be stated aloud to the user before pointing to the saved file, making the two-layer description both visible in chat and persisted on disk.

summary: All 7 acceptance criteria from task-7 are satisfied. Phase 0 contains the taste-profile reading step correctly placed after tokens.json parsing (step 3) and before the checkpoint, with proper graceful degradation for missing/empty file. Phase 2 references taste-profile for layout selection (boldness, anti-patterns). Phase 3 references taste-profile for assembly (colors, typography, anti-patterns). Phase 4 contains two-layer description instruction with Layer 1 (brief) and Layer 2 (detailed), saved to .description.md. Final Check has both new items. All 6 original phases (0-5) are preserved with their headers unchanged. All 6 existing checkpoints are present and unmodified (except Phase 0 which was correctly extended). All 5 existing reference links resolve to existing files. One minor finding: the two-layer description is implemented as a file artifact rather than a conversational presentation pattern per user-spec Scenario G, but this aligns with tech-spec wording and is acceptable.
